diary: Woke up early with that Monday energy, even though the light rail mess had me second-guessing my commute. Coffee tasted better than usual—maybe because of the sun streaming through the kitchen window. Got to the office and knocked out the Q2 metrics review, but honestly, I couldn't stop thinking about the World Cup match. Snuck out at 9:53 to catch USA vs Wales at a café downtown. The place was packed, everyone shouting at the screen. Grabbed a quick lunch after, felt like a kid playing hooky. Afternoon was productive despite the distraction—team meetings went smooth. Hit the climbing gym on my way home and managed a new route that's been kicking my ass for weeks. Something about today just clicked. Finished the evening with handwritten letters to old friends. Haven't done that in months. Felt good, grounding.
thought_process: I needed a mental break today, and the World Cup gave me permission to take it. The light rail closure actually freed me up—no commute stress, no packed trains. I've been grinding through Q2 reports for weeks, so slipping out for the match felt earned rather than reckless. The good weather and that early win against Wales put me in a genuinely good mood, which carried into the climbing gym. I almost skipped it, but I'm glad I didn't. Writing those letters was the cherry on top—been meaning to reconnect with people, and something about the day's lightness made it feel possible. Work got done, but today was really about remembering there's more to life than spreadsheets.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
